mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures current consumer expenditure momentum, which directly translates into corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive following month-over-month RSAFS growth in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate discount rates and debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment (UNRATE) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) generate a stagflationary profit squeeze via falling real demand and rising input costs, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations where both UNRATE and CPIAUCSL increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal an impending output contraction that precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances when the prevailing regime is classified as late-cycle expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect durable goods demand and construction sector strength that lift cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow month-over-month HOUST increases in fewer than 52% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) coinciding with falling housing starts (HOUST) amplify credit-sensitive sector stress and tightening financial conditions, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases and HOUST decreases simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above zero confirms aggregate demand expansion that lifts broad corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns do not follow positive GDPC1 growth in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate tightening labor markets and accelerating household income growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.
